titleEn: Bayer launches new Intacta 5+ soybean trait in Brazil
titleZh: 拜耳在巴西推出Intacta 5+大豆性状技术
summaryZh: 拜耳宣布在巴西推出Intacta 5+大豆种子，该种子耐受五种除草剂（硝磺草酮、麦草畏、草甘膦、草铵膦和2,4-D），并具有抗虫性。商业品种预计2027/28种植季上市，需等待监管批准。巴西是全球第二大生物技术作物生产国，大豆和棉花转基因采用率达99%。该技术被视为应对农业挑战的重要遗传进步。
featuredReason: 虽然该新闻主要聚焦转基因大豆种子技术，与智慧畜牧（IoT/AI/自动化）的直接相关性较低，但作为农业上游关键投入品创新，对饲料原料供应和成本有重要影响，值得畜牧行业人士关注。
contentFull: Markets & policy Market trends Feed Markets Bayer launches new Intacta 5+ soybean trait in Brazil Five-herbicide tolerant seed set for 2027/28 pending approvals 27 November 2025 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media South America German conglomerate Bayer on Wednesday said it will sell a new soybean product called Intacta 5+ for farmers in Brazil, the world's largest exporter and producer of the oilseed, as it seeks to perpetuate the use of genetically engineered seeds in the South American country, reported Reuters. Bayer in a statement said commercial varieties with the new biotechnology are expected to be ready for the 2027/28 crop season, pending regulatory approvals and business decisions in Brazil and abroad.Intacta 5+ seeds are the first technology in Brazil with tolerance to five herbicides: mesotrione, dicamba, glyphosate, glufosinate, and 2,4-D, Bayer said. The new seed also offers protection against certain caterpillars that can damage soy plants, the company added.Brazilian farmers have embraced genetically modified seeds, and their rapid adoption in recent decades has been credited as a major contributor to yield growth for various Brazilian crops.According to a November 2024 report by the US Department of Agriculture's Foreign Agricultural Service, Brazil is the second-largest producer of biotech crops in the world, behind the United States.For the 2024/2025 crop season, the report said Brazil was forecast to sow 68.5 million hectares (169.3 million acres) with genetically engineered traits. Adoption rates for soybeans and cotton have reached 99%, while for corn the rate is 95%, the report said.The new seeds bring "an important genetic advancement that will help farmers face current and future agricultural challenges,” said Marcio Santos, CEO of Bayer's crop division in Brazil.Bayer's Intacta technology is responsible for adding 21.2 million metric tons to soybean production in Brazil over the last 10 years, the company said, citing a study by agribusiness consultancy Agroconsult. translationZh:
德国企业集团拜耳周三表示，将在巴西（全球最大的大豆出口国和生产国）向农民销售一种名为Intacta 5+的新型大豆产品，旨在延续转基因种子在南美国家的使用。据路透社报道，拜耳在一份声明中表示，采用这项新生物技术的商业品种预计将在2027/28种植季准备就绪，但需等待巴西及海外的监管批准和商业决策。

拜耳称，Intacta 5+种子是巴西首个耐受五种除草剂的技术：硝磺草酮、麦草畏、草甘膦、草铵膦和2,4-D。该公司补充说，这种新种子还能提供针对某些可能损害大豆植株的毛虫的保护。

巴西农民已广泛接受转基因种子，近几十年来其快速采用被认为是巴西多种作物产量增长的主要贡献因素。

根据美国农业部外国农业服务局2024年11月的一份报告，巴西是全球第二大生物技术作物生产国，仅次于美国。

该报告称，在2024/2025种植季，巴西预计将播种6850万公顷（1.693亿英亩）的转基因性状作物。大豆和棉花的采用率已达到99%，而玉米的采用率为95%。

拜耳巴西作物部门首席执行官Marcio Santos表示，新种子带来了“一项重要的遗传进步，将帮助农民应对当前和未来的农业挑战”。

拜耳援引农业咨询公司Agroconsult的一项研究称，过去10年间，拜耳的Intacta技术为巴西大豆产量增加了2120万公吨。
contentHtml:
Bayer launches new Intacta 5+ soybean trait in Brazil 
Five-herbicide tolerant seed set for 2027/28 pending approvals
